approval_notes: F001 scope, tech, and licence are already owner-approved via DECISIONS.md (D008, D024, D040), ADR 0005, docs/LICENSING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, and CODEX_TASKS.md task 001. No acceptance steps were altered.

Secret scanning uses detect-secrets and dependency scanning uses pip-audit and npm audit because they are pip/npm installable and runnable in the local and CI environments without new service dependencies.
objective: Establish a clean, reproducible public monorepo baseline with complete licensing and notice files, Python and Node tooling, formatting, linting, tests, secret and dependency scanning, CI that rejects a controlled failure, and focused PR and commit conventions.
scope: Add AGPL-3.0 LICENSE and the attribution and notice files named in docs/LICENSING.md: NOTICE, ADDITIONAL_TERMS.md, TRADEMARKS.md, AUTHORS.md, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, CONTRIBUTING.md.
Add Python tooling in pyproject.toml: ruff lint and format configuration and pytest configuration, plus a repository-baseline pytest suite that asserts repository invariants.
Add Node tooling in package.json: prettier and eslint (flat config) with ignore files and format and lint scripts.
Add reproducibility and pre-commit configuration: .editorconfig, .gitignore, .pre-commit-config.yaml, and a detect-secrets .secrets.baseline.
Add CI at .github/workflows/ci.yml running Python lint/format/test, Node format/lint, secret scan, and dependency audit, with no secrets and no application network access.
Add governance: .github/pull_request_template.md and protected-main plus commit-convention guidance in CONTRIBUTING.md.
Add scripts/check.ps1 and scripts/check.sh that run all F001 checks locally and mirror CI, resolving the repository root from their own path.
out_of_scope: Application scaffolding: FastAPI API, worker, scheduler, React frontend, PostgreSQL, docker-compose (F002 and later).
Configuration system, domain model, Z0 engine, adapters, adviser, LLM routing, deployment (F003+).
Changing feature ledger descriptions, priorities, or acceptance steps.
Merging the pull request, tagging a release, or proceeding to F002.
Adding GitHub Actions that consume secrets, external services, or network access from tests.
acceptance_criteria: The approved AGPL-3.0 LICENSE and every notice file named in docs/LICENSING.md exist and are non-empty.
pyproject.toml configures ruff and pytest; ruff check, ruff format --check, and pytest all pass on the repository.
package.json configures prettier and eslint; prettier --check and eslint pass on tracked sources.
detect-secrets scan reports no unaudited secrets against the committed baseline.
pip-audit runs against declared dev dependencies and npm audit runs against the Node manifest without failing the build on the empty baseline.
scripts/check.ps1 and scripts/check.sh run the full check suite and resolve the repository root from their own path.
A controlled violation (for example a lint error) causes the check suite and CI logic to fail, and removing it makes them pass.
The CI workflow declares no secrets and performs no application network calls.
A fresh independent Level 1 evaluator records results in agent-state/evaluation.json before F001 is marked passing.

end_to_end_steps: From a clean checkout, read the licence and notice files and confirm they exist and are non-empty.
Run scripts/check.ps1 (or scripts/check.sh) and confirm all checks pass.
Introduce a controlled lint violation, confirm the check suite fails, remove it, and confirm the suite passes.
Inspect .github/workflows/ci.yml and confirm it declares no secrets and no application network access.
negative_tests: A deliberate ruff violation must make ruff check and the check suite fail with an actionable message.
An unaudited high-entropy string must make detect-secrets fail until baselined or removed.
Running scripts/check from outside the repository must still resolve the repository root from the script path.

risks.data_migration: None; F001 introduces no application or database state.
risks.security_privacy: Checks must not print secrets or full environment dumps. Secret scanning baseline must not contain real secrets. CI uses no secrets.
risks.portability: Tooling must run on Windows PowerShell and POSIX shells; check scripts resolve the repository root from their own path. Node and Python dev tools are pinned for reproducibility.
risks.zero_cost_safety: No hosting or paid dependency is introduced. No free-tier or Z0 claim is made by F001.
risks.regression: Prettier and ruff formatting must not rewrite hand-authored docs and agent-state files; ignore files scope formatters away from curated content to keep diffs reviewable.

notes: Scope is bounded to the owner-approved F001 foundation deliverables in CODEX_TASKS.md task 001 and docs/LICENSING.md; acceptance steps in the feature ledger are unchanged.
